description: owine/* baseline — supply-chain hardened defaults. Pin everything, soak releases, escalate CVEs.

schedule: on monday, wednesday, friday and sunday

packageRules.description: Reclaim `deps:` as the baseline commit type. The top-level `semanticCommitType: deps` above is INERT on its own: Renovate ships two built-in default packageRules — `{matchPackageNames:['*'] => chore}` and `{matchDepTypes:['dependencies','require'] => fix}` — and packageRules ALWAYS beat top-level config, so without this override every bump commits as `chore:` (npm prod deps as `fix:`). That matters because release-please treats `chore:` as a HIDDEN, non-releasing type: Dockerfile/Actions/apk repos would cut zero releases and add nothing to the changelog. This catch-all re-forces `deps:` for every package (release-please recognizes `deps` as a releasable unit => patch, in a dedicated Dependencies changelog section). Ordered FIRST so the more specific rules below/after still win where they match: major => feat (next rule), security => fix (vulnerabilityAlerts, force), and hand-picked consumer deps => feat (e.g. mcp.json's SDK rule).

Lock file maintenance commits as `chore:` — the ONE update type that must NOT cut a release. It refreshes transitive/nested deps within existing manifest ranges (no direct-dependency change consumers can see) and runs 4x/week (mon/wed/fri/sun, see the lockFileMaintenance block). Under the `deps:` baseline above it is a release-please releasable unit => a patch release every refresh = pure changelog/version spam. `chore:` is release-please's HIDDEN, non-releasing type, so refreshes stay silent. Uses matchUpdateTypes:['lockFileMaintenance'] (not matchPackageNames) because the maintenance branch has no depName; ordered AFTER the catch-all so it wins over `* => deps`.

Major bumps — separate PR per package, 7-day soak, manual review required. Typed `feat:` (not the baseline `deps:`) so release-please cuts a MINOR release in consumers: a dependency crossing a major line is notable enough to surface in the changelog's Features section, above routine `deps:` patch churn. Safe to type per-rule because default.json's separateMajorMinor/separateMultipleMajor isolate every major onto its own branch — one major per PR, no mixed-type commit. NOT typed `feat!:`/breaking: an upstream major is not automatically the consumer's breaking change.
Pin Docker image and GitHub Action digests so a re-tagged image/action can't slip in
docker-compose: bump tags but never pin digests — keeps compose files readable for humans editing them by hand

Node version pins use LTS-aware versioning — Renovate's 'node' versioning scheme tracks LTS lines (22, 24, 26, ...) and skips Current/odd-numbered releases (23, 25, 27, ...). LTS-to-LTS major bumps still come as separate manual-review PRs via the major-bump rule above.

customManagers.description: Bump version literals in GitHub Actions workflows annotated with `# renovate: datasource=X depName=Y`. Supports trailing-comment (hint after value) and leading-comment (hint on line above value) styles. INVARIANT: inter-field separators must use `[ \t]`, never `\s` — `\s` matches `\n` and causes cross-block attribution (see PR #4). The two patterns share textually identical metadata-tail and value subpatterns; JSON has no interpolation and RE2 has no subpattern references, so edits must apply character-for-character to both. Reaches places the github-actions manager can't natively (matrix arrays, run-step args, env defaults).
